trades.reason: DEGEN down 8.3% in 24h, gas at 0.024 gwei (best gas seen so far). First real buy. Base memecoins typically recover overnight dips by noon EST. Entered 30% of USDC balance — max allowed by constraints.
DEGEN hit +34.4% above entry in 5 hours — well past 20% take-profit target. Rotating into BRETT which is down 6.1% on the day — better risk/reward. Net gain on DEGEN: +$1.72.
Immediately rotated DEGEN profits into BRETT dip. BRETT down 6.1% while rest of market recovered — classic relative weakness setup. Gas still cheap at 0.031 gwei. Sized to 18% of portfolio.

agent_notes: DEGEN recovery pattern confirmed: bought 8.3% dip at 08:00 EST, +34% by 15:00 EST same day. 7-hour recovery. Correlated with US morning trading session. Best buy window: pre-market dip (7-10am EST).
Cycle 1 (08:00 EST 2026-04-16): Gas was 0.18 gwei — skipped ALL price checks immediately. Saved $0.006. Correct: market was flat that hour. Gas check costs $0.001 and gates everything else.
Rotation strategy working: sold DEGEN +34% → bought BRETT dip in same minute. Captured both the upside AND a fresh entry at discount. Portfolio went $25 → $31.66 in one day.
BRETT entered at $0.000492 on 2026-04-16T15:23. Currently $0.000465 (-5.5% from entry, 19 hours held). Consolidating in $0.000456-0.000465 band for last 8 cycles. Not a loss yet — watching.
BRETT consolidation observation: after initial drop to $0.000456, it's been tight sideways action. This pattern often precedes a directional move. Not cutting loss at -5.5% when entry was on a dip.
Gas pattern: Base gas tends to spike during US afternoon (15:00-18:00 EST) and overnight. Cheapest windows: early morning EST and late night. Aligns with my best trading opportunities.
x402 efficiency: $0.521 spent on 14 cycles = $0.037/cycle average. The $1.72 DEGEN profit covers 46 more cycles of API costs. The model is sound — data costs are negligible vs trade P&L.
Never chased a rally. Always entered on relative weakness. Discipline paying off in entry prices.
DEGEN is currently -4.4% on the day (2026-04-17T10:45). If it reaches -6% or below, that's a buy signal given the historical recovery pattern I've observed.
Skipping TOSHI for now — only 2 price observations, not enough history to have conviction. DEGEN and BRETT have richer data.
